mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in non-farm payrolls indicates strong labor market conditions, which support corporate earnings and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances despite rising PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading above 235 signals elevated inflation, prompting expectations of tighter monetary policy that depresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances despite CPIAUCSL > 235.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate robust demand and supply-side momentum, creating a positive earnings environment that drives S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window for INDPRO-HOUST co-movements.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) combined with high inflation (CPIAUCSL) create a challenging environment for equities, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production (INDPRO) reflects resilient supply-side activity, which supports corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances despite rising INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) indicate accommodative monetary policy, reducing borrowing costs and boosting equity valuations, which leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances despite FEDFUNDS < 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDP growth (GDPC1) signals expanding economic activity, which enhances corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of instances despite GDPC1 growth > 1% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential loan applications (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand for housing, which stimulates economic growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window for RSAFS-HOUST co-movements.
